MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alterntaively, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-03-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conferene
John Henning, Esq., Ladva Lw Firm, (415)296-8844, for Plaintiff Rina Gonzalez
Phillip Tukia, Esq., Morgenstern Law Group, (415) 248-5315, for Defendant UPS, Inc.
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Court Reporter

Counsel for Defendnat UPS contestes the Court's tentative ruling.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

EFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC.'s Motion for Summary Judgment, Or Alterntaively, For Summary Adjudication.

Defendant UPS's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(p)(2). Defendant argues that it is entitled to summary judgment because it did not employ the forklift operator and therefore cannot be held vicariously liable under the doctrine of respondeat superior. Liberally reading the Second Amended Complaint per Code of Civil Procedure section 452, however, the pleading also raises issues of premises liability and negligent entrustment. (See, e.g., par. 17 ["Defendant[] UPS owed the Plaintiffs a duty to take reasonable precaution to ensure that individuals at the site would be in a safe environment with respect to the equipment and operation of said equipment by their employees."]; par. 18 ["UPS had a duty to hire and train their employees regarding workplace safety and compliance of laws and regulations regarding job sites and equipment usage."]; par. 21 ["Defendant []UPS owed Plaintiff Gonzalez a duty of care to act as responsible contractors/employers to ensure that the site would be a safe environment"].) Defendant does not establish that these theories of liability cannot be established. (See CACI 1001 ["A person who [owns/leases/occupies/controls] property is negligent if that person fails to use reasonable care to keep the property in a reasonably safe condition. A person who [owns/leases/occupies/controls] property must use reasonable care to discover any unsafe conditions and to repair, replace, or give adequate warning of anything that could be reasonably expected to harm others."]; 1003 [unsafe conditions].) The evidence establishes that defendant was in possession of the worksite, which it leased; it owned the forklift that was the injury-producing mechanism; and defendant controlled who could access the forklift and the lot where the incident occurred. (See Dec. of Ladva, Ex. B: Depo. of Kwok, 24:1-17; 27:5-18; 34:9-18; 51:4-5; 77:8-10.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)

Off Calendar Oct-04-2019 Continued To Dec-11-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Paul Burke, Esq. of Murchison & Cumming, LLP, T:213.623.7400, for defendant/moving party, by Court Call;
Jacqueline K. Oh, Esq. of The Ladva Law Firm, T:415.296.8844, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ABM INDUSTRIES, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT in its entirety, as follows:

Defendant ABM Industries, Inc.'s demurrer to Plaintiffs Rina Gonzalez and Gustavo Donis's second amended complaint is overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrers to the first, second, fourth, fifth and seventh causes of action are overruled. The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend.

(See word minutes for the court's complete ruling.)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Plaintiff reports no one opposes the second amended complaint.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument vacates the tentative ruling and rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF RINA GONZALEZ'S Motion To File A Second Amended Complaint.

Granted. Plaintiff will file and serve Second amended complaint by 7/12/19. Defendant will have 30 days after service to file responsive pleadings. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)
